title: populus × robusta
intro: Populus × robusta, also known as Hybrid Black Cottonwood or Black Cottonwood, is a hybrid plant species derived from the cross between Populus trichocarpa and Populus heterophylla, commonly found in North America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, making it useful for pain relief and reducing inflammation. The health benefits of Populus × robusta include its ability to lower fevers, alleviate rheumatism, and act as an antiseptic. The plant is traditionally prepared for medicinal use through decoctions, infusions, or tinctures, and its bark is often dried and used in herbal supplements or teas.
